6358db348e48451b24cfad92.question: A variety of these beings derives from mugwort puppets created to fight the smallpox demon. These beings name a temple in the Matsugaya area of Tōkyō near a street with many kitchen supply shops. A variety of these beings native to Kyūshū that sheds its hair in bathtubs is called the hyōsube. These beings ask their unborn babies if they want to be aborted in a story narrated by (*) Number 23, a psychiatric patient. These beings seek balls of energy called shirikodama that they extract from people’s anuses. For 10 points, name these yōkai whose heads have dishshaped depressions filled with water.
6358db348e48451b24cfad92.question_sanitized: A variety of these beings derives from mugwort puppets created to fight the smallpox demon. These beings name a temple in the Matsugaya area of Tokyo near a street with many kitchen supply shops. A variety of these beings native to Kyushu that sheds its hair in bathtubs is called the hyosube. These beings ask their unborn babies if they want to be aborted in a story narrated by (*) Number 23, a psychiatric patient. These beings seek balls of energy called shirikodama that they extract from people's anuses. For 10 points, name these yokai whose heads have dishshaped depressions filled with water.
6358db348e48451b24cfad92.answer: kappa [accept mintuci or mintsuchi; accept hyōsube before “hyōsube”; prompt on yōkai] {The story is Kappa by Akutagawa Ryūnosuke.}
